palette.name: Burn Orange
palette.usage: Primary brand color. All primary CTAs, highlights, active states, section accents, progress fills. The defining color of the aesthetic.
palette.avoid: Never use on orange-adjacent warm surfaces. Never pair with blue or green.

palette.name: Void Black
palette.usage: Primary background. All page and card backgrounds. Preserves maximum contrast.

palette.name: Signal Yellow
palette.usage: Warning states, caution labels, secondary highlights. Warning stripes paired with black. Restricted zone markers. Secondary channel of glitch effect.
palette.avoid: Never use as body text. High saturation.

palette.name: Alert Red
palette.usage: Danger filled backgrounds, emergency states, destructive action buttons. Use for large filled surfaces.
palette.avoid: Do not use for purely decorative purposes.

palette.name: Hot Red
palette.usage: Active and live danger states, alert glows, text and outlines over dark backgrounds. Primary channel of glitch effect.
palette.avoid: Do not use on large areas. Reserve for text, borders, glows.

palette.name: Aged White
palette.usage: Primary text color on all dark backgrounds. Warm tint avoids the clinical sterility of pure white. Aged paper feel.
palette.avoid: Do not use #FFFFFF for white. Pure white contradicts the industrial theme with a clinical outlook.

palette.name: Soot
palette.usage: Elevated surfaces, card backgrounds, component wells.
palette.avoid: Do not use for borders or it will mix with VOID BLACK. Not suitable for text.

palette.name: Graphite
palette.usage: All borders, dividers, table rules, and default component outlines. The universal line color.
palette.avoid: Never use as text color. Not suitable as a background.

palette.name: Slag
palette.usage: Secondary labels, metadata text, serial/barcode numbers, placeholder values, status subtext.
palette.avoid: Do not use for primary labels, CTAs, or anything that requires the primary focus.

fonts.name: Barlow Condensed
rules: The default weight for UI is 700. Go heavier (800-900) for impact.

fonts.name: Share Tech Mono
rules: Purely technical contexts only. Never for headlines or UI labels.
Serial numbers, timestamps, hex values, system readouts, barcode labels.
Color: --slag (#666660) for secondary data, Burn Orange for highlighted data.

scale.name: Display
scale.usage: Hero titles, section openers, poster headlines. One per composition.

scale.name: Headline XL
scale.usage: Major section headings or secondary titles.

scale.name: Headline LG
scale.usage: Sub-section headings, card titles, feature callouts. Typically in Burn Orange.

scale.name: Label MD
scale.usage: Component labels, nav items, form labels, table headers.

scale.name: Label SM
scale.usage: Secondary labels, metadata, small callouts. Typicall in Slag.

scale.name: Mono / Serial
scale.usage: All technical text. Serial numbers, barcodes, timestamps, hex codes, system classifications.
